hallucinations.description: Multiple references cite papers with future publication years (2025, 2026) or arXiv IDs that do not correspond to real publications. These cannot be verified and appear fabricated.
hallucinations.evidence: Fan et al. (2026) arXiv:2602.11217; Khanmohammadi et al. (2025) EMNLP; Lunardi et al. (2025) ECAI; Plaut et al. (2024) TMLR 2025.

hallucinations.description: Paper claims use of mixed-effects logistic regression with random intercepts per benchmark, but the provided code implements only standard (non-mixed) logistic regression via statsmodels.Logit and sklearn.LogisticRegression.
hallucinations.evidence: Section 3: 'A mixed-effects logistic regression is fitted: logit(P(flip_i = 1)) = β₀ + β₁ · margin_i + u_{d(i)}'. Code in analysis_pipeline.py: fit_logistic_regression uses sm.Logit without random effects.

hallucinations.description: Precise numerical results for H-E1 (AUROC values, β₁ = −4.3295, flip rate 12.5%, η² = 0.289) are reported but unsupported by the experiment artifacts; the only experiment_results.json present is for anisotropy (H-M1), and H-E1 logs show no matching output.
hallucinations.evidence: Table 1 reports AUROC 0.8668/0.8034/0.9086 and β₁ = −4.3295 for pair2; h-e1/terminal.log and experiment_results.json contain only H-M1 anisotropy numbers (e.g., ratio=2.8996).

overall_assessment: Hallucinations found: nonexistent citations, mismatched methodology description vs. code, and likely fabricated H-E1 numerical results. H-M1/H-M2 results are more consistent with logs but overall paper contains multiple fabrications.
